---
title: "Browse"
subtitle: "Every article in the catalogue, sortable and searchable"
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import html
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
from itables import show as itables_show
import itables.options as itables_opt

itables_opt.classes = "display nowrap compact"
itables_opt.maxBytes = 0

DERIV = Path("data/derivatives")
records = json.loads((DERIV / "articles.json").read_text())
df = pd.DataFrame.from_records(records)
```

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
Use the search box (top right of the table) to filter by any text — title,
author, topic, year. Click column headers to sort. Click a title to open the
article on royalsocietypublishing.org.
:::

```{python}
#| label: tbl-browse
def linkify(title, url, doi):
    label = html.escape(title or "(untitled)")
    href = None
    if isinstance(url, str) and url:
        href = url
    elif isinstance(doi, str) and doi:
        href = f"https://doi.org/{doi}"
    if href:
        return f'<a href="{html.escape(href)}" target="_blank" rel="noopener">{label}</a>'
    return label

def join_authors(authors):
    if not authors:
        return ""
    return "; ".join(authors)

table = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "Year": df["year"],
        "Topic": df["topic"],
        "Title": [linkify(t, u, d) for t, u, d in zip(df["title"], df["canonical_url"], df["doi"])],
        "Authors": [join_authors(a) for a in df["authors"]],
        "Pages": df["n_pages"],
        "DOI": df["doi"],
    }
)
table = table.sort_values(["Year", "Title"], na_position="last").reset_index(drop=True)

itables_show(
    table,
    lengthMenu=[25, 50, 100, 250],
    paging=True,
    columnDefs=[
        {"targets": 0, "width": "70px"},
        {"targets": 4, "width": "70px"},
    ],
)
```
